name: Check `n Go
description_short: At 13338 E Jefferson Ave, Detroit, MI 48215, Check `n Go can be reached at +1 313-331-5500.
description_long:
Check 'n Go is a storefront lending company operating a physical location at 13338 East Jefferson Avenue in Detroit, Michigan. The company specializes in short-term emergency lending products designed to help consumers cover unexpected expenses or bridge gaps until their next paycheck. They have been in operation long enough to build a significant customer review base on Google (5.0 stars with 1,031 reviews), suggesting established local market presence.

The company offers payday loans as their primary product—short-term loans repaid by the borrower's next payday. They also provide installment loans, which represent a step toward more structured repayment compared to traditional payday products. Additionally, Check 'n Go offers Netspend Prepaid Mastercard® services for unbanked or underbanked customers. Their application process can be completed online, in-store, or over the phone where available, with funding typically provided the next business day for online applications or same-day for in-store applications.

Check 'n Go distinguishes itself through rapid approval and funding timelines, straightforward application requirements, and willingness to lend to customers with bad or no credit history. The Detroit location maintains extended business hours (10 AM–6 PM weekdays, 10 AM–2 PM Saturday) and has received specific employee recognition in customer reviews for professional service quality. They explicitly state that "No Cost Extended Payment Plans may be available," suggesting some flexibility in repayment structures.

However, prospective borrowers should note that this is a high-cost lending product. The website provides no APR, fee, or rate information, which is typical of payday lenders but problematic for consumer transparency. The company cannot advise how loans affect credit scores and does not clearly disclose whether credit inquiries are soft or hard pulls. This remains a last-resort borrowing option best suited for genuine emergencies when other sources are unavailable.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Same-day funding available for in-store applications
Accepts applicants with bad or no credit history
Simple application requirements (ID, proof of income, active checking account)
Extended weekday hours (10 AM–6 PM) including Saturday service
High Google review rating (5.0 stars, 1,031 reviews) with positive employee feedback
No Cost Extended Payment Plans may be available for qualifying borrowers
cons: No APR, interest rate, or fee information disclosed on website, making true cost comparison impossible
Short-term payday product structure can create debt cycles requiring repeat borrowing
Requires active checking account open for minimum 30 days, excluding newly banked consumers
Website currently showing maintenance message, limiting ability to apply online at time of review
No mention of credit reporting to bureaus, limiting ability to build credit through repayment
best_for: Employed individuals with immediate cash needs who have access to a bank account
Consumers with poor or no credit history unable to qualify for traditional personal loans
Borrowers seeking same-day or next-day funding for unexpected expenses
People without access to credit cards or other emergency credit options
diagnosis: Check 'n Go is best for employed individuals with bad or no credit who need emergency cash within 24 hours and have an active checking account. The primary caveat is that payday loans are high-cost debt products with rates and fees not disclosed on the website; this should only be used as a last resort when other borrowing options are unavailable, as the short-term structure frequently leads to repeat borrowing cycles.
services: Payday loans (short-term loans repaid by next payday)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
